Title: Simultaneous regulation of F5H in COMT-RNAi transgenic switchgrass alters effects of COMT suppression on syringyl lignin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch-point to S-lignin biosynthesis; key hydroxylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates coniferaldehyde and coniferyl alcohol at the 5-position to generate 5-OH intermediates used for syringyl lignin formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Appears to be a key control/rate-limiting step for S lignin biosynthesis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polymer influencing biomass recalcitrance, forage digestibility, bioethanol conversion, and pulping efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in well-lignified tissues and organs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in well-lignified tissues and organs; stems at R1 stage analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth, 16 h light, stems collected at R1 stage
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from Phytozome v4.1; switchgrass Gene Expression Atlas (probeset AP13ITG56842_at); LC-MS/MS soluble phenolics profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; overexpression; qRT-PCR; lignin thioacidolysis; LC-MS/MS phenolic profiling; analysis in COMT-RNAi background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulation decreased S units and 5-OH G units while increasing G units; overexpression increased S units and 5-OH G units while decreasing G units; in COMT-RNAi backgrounds, F5H down-regulation further reduced S lignin, whereas F5H overexpression partially or fully restored S lignin depending on COMT suppression strength.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvF5H is the switchgrass ortholog functioning in syringyl lignin biosynthesis. 2. PvF5H RNAi reduced S lignin by 12.9%–35.4% and increased G lignin by 4.2%–22.5%. 3. PvF5H overexpression increased S lignin by 17.4%–22.5% and reduced G lignin by 15.3%–34.7%. 4. In severe COMT-RNAi plants, F5H down-regulation synergistically worsened S lignin deficiency, while F5H overexpression partially compensated it. 5. In moderate COMT-RNAi plants, F5H overexpression fully restored S lignin levels.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious biomass penalty or developmental defect; effects on saccharification depended on COMT background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful lignin-engineering target for tuning S/G ratio and biomass digestibility; should be combined cautiously with COMT manipulation because F5H up-regulation can antagonize COMT-suppression effects on lowering S lignin, while F5H down-regulation can synergistically enhance digestibility in severe COMT-RNAi backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PvF5H expression is positively correlated with PvCOMT. A relatively low degree of F5H down-regulation was sufficient to alter S lignin strongly, suggesting tighter control than COMT.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream methylation step in S-lignin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 5-hydroxylated monolignol intermediates to form syringyl lignin precursors.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme for S lignin biosynthesis, but not rate-limiting relative to F5H in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polymer influencing biomass recalcitrance, forage digestibility, bioethanol conversion, and pulping efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PvCOMT1 shows ~200-fold higher signal intensity than PvCOMT2 across tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in switchgrass tissues; stems at R1 stage analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth, 16 h light, stems collected at R1 stage
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene Expression Atlas (COMT probe KanlowCTG00989_s_at); LC-MS/MS soluble phenolics profiling; crude enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; lignin profiling; LC-MS/MS detection of 5-OH coniferyl alcohol glycoside; crude enzyme activity assay; combination with F5H RNAi/OE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: COMT down-regulation reduced S lignin, increased 5-OH G units, and improved saccharification; severe suppression caused stronger effects than moderate suppression. Its effects were modified antagonistically or synergistically by F5H overexpression or suppression, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCOMT1 is the major COMT isoform expressed in switchgrass relative to PvCOMT2. 2. COMT-RNAi reduced S lignin and caused accumulation of 5-OH G units. 3. F5H down-regulation in COMT-RNAi plants further decreased S units and increased G units. 4. F5H overexpression in COMT-RNAi plants increased 5-OH flux and partially or fully restored S lignin depending on residual COMT activity. 5. COMT crude extracts from RNAi plants showed elevated turnover efficiency at higher substrate concentrations, supporting substrate-compensation logic.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No biomass loss reported; brown stem coloration in COMT-suppressed backgrounds; metabolic accumulation of 5-OH coniferyl alcohol glycoside
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for reducing lignin recalcitrance and improving saccharification, but deployment should consider F5H status because endogenous or engineered F5H up-regulation can offset COMT-suppression effects on S lignin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two isoforms exist, PvCOMT1 and PvCOMT2; PvCOMT1 was targeted because it is much more highly expressed. Discussion notes possible post-translational regulation by phosphorylation known in poplar, but not tested here.
